skill.name: compliance-checker
skill.description: Assess application code against security compliance standards, privacy controls, and secure coding practices.
skill.summary: Review code for OWASP, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, and secure coding gaps.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk tokens, including eval, exec, fetch, process.env, fs.readFileSync, weak crypto, and shell-like markdown fences. Manual review found these are documentation examples and compliance checklists inside SKILL.md, not executable skill logic or instructions to exfiltrate data. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged dangerous tokens across SKILL.md, but the reviewed lines are markdown checklists, code examples, and sample report text. These examples teach users how to identify insecure code and are not executable skill logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The risky strings appear inside fenced markdown or illustrative JavaScript examples. No executable companion script or runtime hook is present in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Illustrative Network and Environment Access
low_findings.description: The fetch calls, hardcoded URLs, and process.env references are shown as examples for SSRF, subresource integrity, encryption key handling, and production error behavior. They do not send data or read real secrets as part of the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context around each line labels the code as compliant or noncompliant detection material. I found no evidence of credential exfiltration or operational network behavior.

low_findings.title: Illustrative Filesystem Read Pattern
low_findings.description: The fs.readFileSync occurrence appears in a compliance-as-code example that explains how an automated checker might read files. It is not packaged as runnable code in this skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem call is embedded in a markdown example under best practices. No evidence found that the skill executes it or requires file access beyond user-requested review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic Code Execution Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md contains eval and exec examples that demonstrate insecure patterns to detect. They are dangerous patterns in sample code but not active execution paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding sections explicitly label these snippets as violations. The evidence supports a documentation false positive rather than real RCE behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptography Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md names MD5, SHA1, DES, and a DES cipher example as weak cryptography to help users identify noncompliant code. This is advisory content, not implementation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The weak crypto references are presented under cryptographic failures with explicit violation labels. No evidence found that the skill uses these algorithms.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Request Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md includes fetch and CDN URL examples for SSRF and subresource integrity review. These examples do not run by themselves.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are inside fenced JavaScript examples and describe vulnerable and compliant patterns. No evidence found of active outbound requests.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Read Example
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md shows fs.readFileSync in a sample automated compliance rule checker. It is not a distributed script or command handler.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file read appears only in documentation. The scanner found one file, SKILL.md, and no runnable JavaScript file.

content.value_statement: Security and privacy requirements are hard to map to application code. This skill guides structured reviews against OWASP, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, and secure coding controls.
actual_capabilities: Maps code findings to OWASP Top 10 categories and common secure coding controls.
Checks examples against PCI-DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, SOC 2, CCPA, FERPA, and ISO 27001 topics.
Produces structured compliance reports with severity, affected areas, and remediation steps.
Reviews data protection practices such as encryption, retention, minimization, and logging.
Highlights authentication, access control, injection, SSRF, and misconfiguration risks.
Supports compliance-focused prompts for full applications, folders, or targeted standards.
limitations: It does not replace a formal legal, privacy, or compliance certification review.
It relies on the code and context provided by the user during the session.
It may need current regulatory interpretation from qualified counsel for high-stakes decisions.
It provides guidance and examples, not an independently verified compliance score.

use_cases.title: Pre-release Security Review
use_cases.description: Review application changes against OWASP and secure coding controls before release.

use_cases.title: Regulated Product Gap Analysis
use_cases.description: Identify privacy and compliance gaps for products handling payment, health, or personal data.

use_cases.title: Developer Remediation Planning
use_cases.description: Convert compliance findings into prioritized fixes with clear implementation guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic OWASP Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small feature or pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code for OWASP Top 10 risks. Summarize each issue, severity, evidence, and recommended fix.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Regulated Data Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused review for sensitive data handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this data flow for GDPR and HIPAA concerns. Focus on collection, storage, logging, access control, retention, and deletion.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Remediation Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn findings into a practical implementation plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a compliance remediation plan for these findings. Group work by priority, affected standard, owner, and expected verification evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Full Application
prompt_templates.prompt: Assess this application against OWASP Top 10, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, and GDPR. Provide a gap analysis, risk summary, control mapping, and remediation roadmap.

output_examples.input: Review an API route that returns patient records after authentication only.
output_examples.output: The report flags missing authorization as a high-risk access control issue. It recommends verifying record ownership or role permission before returning data.

output_examples.input: Check a registration form that collects name, email, date of birth, and Social Security number.
output_examples.output: The review identifies possible GDPR data minimization concerns. It recommends removing fields that are not required for the stated service purpose.

output_examples.input: Assess application logging for a healthcare workflow.
output_examples.output: The assessment highlights PHI exposure in logs and recommends logging user IDs, event types, timestamps, and outcomes instead of clinical details.

best_practices: Provide the relevant code, data flow, and target compliance standard in the same request.
Ask for evidence-based findings with severity, affected controls, and concrete remediation steps.
Use the output as review preparation, then validate conclusions with security and compliance owners.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the generated report as a formal certification or legal determination.
Do not ask for a pass or fail score without providing application context and evidence.
Do not remediate only checklist items while ignoring business logic and data flow risks.

faq.question: Does this skill certify compliance?
faq.answer: No. It helps prepare compliance reviews and gap analyses, but it does not provide formal certification.

faq.question: Which standards does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers OWASP Top 10, OWASP ASVS, CWE, SANS, PCI-DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, SOC 2, CCPA, FERPA, NIST, and ISO 27001 topics.

faq.question: Can it review a full repository?
faq.answer: Yes, when the tool has access to the repository and the user asks for a repository-level review.

faq.question: Can it find privacy issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review data collection, logging, retention, consent, deletion, and sensitive data handling patterns.

faq.question: Does it execute scans automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides review guidance and report structure. Tool execution depends on the host assistant and user permission.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Security engineers, developers, product managers, and compliance teams can use it to structure application compliance reviews.
